title: Weak Airflow | Tampa, FL
hero: Weak airflow is a capacity killer before it is a comfort complaint—static pressure and mass flow at the coil beat guessing with charge gauges.
summary_30s: Treat weak supply as duct, filter, blower, and coil loading in that order of cheap signal. High static with clean filter points to return or flex; wet coil loading spikes pressure and kills latent handling in Tampa humidity.

quick_checks.label: Filter and cabinet
quick_checks.action: Correct filter size and direction; door sealed; no collapsed media in bypass.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: High static mimics every downstream fault including freeze-ups.

quick_checks.label: Register and return balance
quick_checks.action: Closed registers, blocked beds, or choked secondary returns—open and retest.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Bypassed returns pull hot attic air and overload blower.

quick_checks.label: Flex runs
quick_checks.action: Inspect for kinks, sharp bends, and disconnected collars.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Collapsed flex can drop whole-home CFM with a clean filter.

quick_checks.label: Blower speed tap / ECM signal
quick_checks.action: Verify speed call matches cooling profile; wrong tap murders airflow.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Low speed drives coil freeze and liquid floodback.

quick_checks.label: Wet coil face
quick_checks.action: Visual loading; note drain behavior under long runtimes.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Biofilm matting raises pressure and destroys sensible capacity.

safe_diy: Replace loaded filter with correct MERV for grille size.
Open all registers; move furniture blocking returns.
Clear obvious flex kink if accessible without tearing liner.
call_pro_if: Static above manufacturer window after filter fix.
Blower wheel heavy buildup requiring pull and clean.
Duct redesign needed for undersized return.
Ice on indoor coil with weak airflow—do not keep running.
ECM module faults or rewiring beyond homeowner scope.

nodes.label: Complaint: weak supply or poor throw
Filter and cabinet path clean and sealed
Coil deep clean or pan/secondary issues

flow_explanations.A: Quantify weak—room to room or measured static if available.
flow_explanations.B: Wrong MERV on small return is the silent static bomb.
flow_explanations.C: One closed return in a starved system dominates pressure.
flow_explanations.D: Flex is the first mechanical failure mode in attics.
flow_explanations.E: PSC wrong tap or ECM signal fault looks like duct issue.
flow_explanations.F: Wet coil raises pressure and humidity complaints together.
flow_explanations.G: Some homes need return addition—not more refrigerant.

top_causes.why_it_happens: Face area too small for chosen MERV raises static.
top_causes.risk: Coil freeze and compressor slugging follow.

top_causes.why_it_happens: Attic heat and foot traffic kink flex runs.
top_causes.risk: Pulls attic air and loses delivered CFM to rooms.

top_causes.why_it_happens: Dust matting and biofilm add pressure drop.
top_causes.risk: Misdiagnosed as low charge; adding gas worsens floodback.

top_causes.why_it_happens: Return grille area cannot match supply.
top_causes.risk: Chronic high static burns blower motors.

top_causes.why_it_happens: Wrong tap or degraded ECM performance.
top_causes.risk: Low airflow across cooling coil in humid load.

repair_matrix.fix: Pull and clean wheel; verify balance
Chemical or mechanical clean per condition

how_system_works: The blower moves a fixed volume relationship against system pressure. Filters, coils, ducts, and grilles are resistors in series—any choke drops delivered air and raises fan power and noise.

homeowner: Tape for temporary flex support only if safe

repair_if: Localized kink, dirty wheel, coil clean, or damper fault
Filter strategy corrected with measured improvement
replace_if: Motor repeatedly failing on chronic high static until duct fixed
Rusted collapsing trunk beyond economical repair

local_context: Attic ducts in Tampa bake; liner delamination and kinks spike after one season of storage stacking.
field_insight: If noise went up when ‘air got weak,’ think static before leak—homeowners confuse blower struggle with low refrigerant.
maintenance: Keep attic walk paths off flex
cost_pressure: Return mods finished-wall cost more than open-bay attic work.
decision_moment: If TESP is unknown, do not authorize coil clean alone as ‘the fix’—measure before and after.
cta_primary: Book static mapping and a written CFM plan—not guesswork with parts.
cta_secondary: Ice on indoor coil with weak air—shut cooling and call; liquid floodback damages compressor.
stop_diy: Do not keep running with ice on the coil; do not remove filters long-term as a ‘solution.’
